title: Best Prop Trading Firms For Beginners
meta_title: Best Prop Firms for Beginners in 2026 | Top 3 Picks
meta_description: FTMO ($155/mo, 90% split), Apex ($147/mo, 100% first $25K), and Topstep ($165/mo) compared for beginners — rules, drawdowns, and our top pick.
excerpt: FTMO, Apex Trader Funding, and Topstep reviewed for beginners — prices, evaluation rules, profit splits, and which firm to start with in 2026.
content:
Getting funded as a trader sounds straightforward — pass an evaluation, trade the firm's capital, keep a cut of the profits. But for beginners, choosing the wrong prop firm means wasted evaluation fees, confusing rules, and unnecessary failures. The structures, drawdown limits, and evaluation phases vary dramatically between firms.

This guide covers the three firms that consistently stand out for beginners right now: FTMO, Apex Trader Funding, and Topstep. We've evaluated each on evaluation difficulty, profit splits, pricing, and how forgiving the rules are when you're still learning the ropes.

What Makes a Prop Firm Beginner-Friendly?

Not all prop firms are designed with new traders in mind. Some are built for experienced traders chasing aggressive profit targets. Others include guardrails that give beginners a realistic shot at getting funded without burning through evaluation fees on repeated failed attempts.

Here's what separates a beginner-friendly firm from one that will drain your wallet before you ever see a funded account:

  • Simple evaluation structure: One-phase evaluations are far easier to navigate than multi-step challenges. Fewer hurdles mean fewer ways to fail.
  • No daily drawdown limits: A single volatile session can wipe out an evaluation that has a daily drawdown cap. Firms without one give you more room to recover from bad days.
  • Free trial or demo: The ability to test the platform and rules before paying is a significant advantage. It removes the guesswork and lets you fail safely.
  • Challenge fee refunds: Some firms refund your evaluation fee on first payout, meaningfully reducing your total financial risk.
  • Reasonable profit targets: A 10% target over 30 days is achievable. A 20% target in 14 days is a trap for beginners.
  • Educational resources: Does the firm explain its rules clearly? Is there a community or support structure to help you understand where you went wrong?

Quick Comparison: Best Prop Firms for Beginners in 2026

Firm Rating Starting Price Profit Split Evaluation Phases Markets
FTMO 4.5/5 $155/mo Up to 90% 2-phase Forex, crypto, indices, commodities
Apex Trader Funding 4.3/5 $147/mo 100% of first $25K 1-phase Futures only
Topstep 4.2/5 $165/mo 100% of first $5K, then 90% 1-phase Futures only

Top 3 Prop Firms for Beginners: In-Depth Reviews

1. FTMO — Best Overall for Forex Beginners

Rating: 4.5/5 | Starting at $155/mo

FTMO is the most recognized name in prop trading, and that reputation is earned. Founded in 2014, it has paid out hundreds of millions in trader profits and remains the gold standard for forex prop trading. For beginners, the biggest draw is the free trial — you can run through the full evaluation process without paying a cent, which is invaluable when you're learning what rules you need to follow.

The FTMO Challenge is a two-phase evaluation. Phase 1 requires a 10% profit target; Phase 2 requires 5%. You get 30 days for each phase with no minimum trading days. Once you pass both, you become a funded FTMO Trader with access to accounts ranging from $10,000 to $200,000.

The 90% profit split is industry-leading — most competitors cap out at 80%. FTMO also refunds your challenge fee on your first payout, so your net cost is effectively zero if you pass on the first attempt. That fee refund policy meaningfully lowers the financial risk for beginners who need a few attempts to dial in their approach.

The main challenge: the two-phase structure and strict drawdown rules (5% daily, 10% overall) can be punishing. One bad session can end your evaluation entirely. But FTMO's account analysis tools help you understand exactly where you went wrong, which accelerates learning in a way that many cheaper firms don't.

Best for: Forex traders and disciplined beginners who want the best long-term payout structure and are willing to invest time studying the platform rules before paying.

Read our full FTMO review →

2. Apex Trader Funding — Best for Futures Beginners on a Budget

Rating: 4.3/5 | Starting at $147/mo

If you trade futures, Apex Trader Funding has arguably the most beginner-friendly evaluation structure available right now. Its one-step evaluation removes the stress of a second challenge phase, and — most importantly — there is no daily drawdown limit.

That last feature is a bigger deal than it might seem. Daily drawdown limits are the single most common cause of failed evaluations for new traders. A volatile session, a news event, a bad fill — any of these can breach a daily limit and end your challenge immediately. Apex removes that trap entirely, giving beginners more room to recover from rough days without losing the entire evaluation.

The 100% profit split on your first $25,000 in profits is exceptional — you keep every dollar until you've taken out $25K, then shift to a 90/10 split in your favor. At $147/month for the entry-level plan, it's also the most affordable of our top three picks.

The key limitation is market access: Apex is futures only. No forex, no equities, no crypto. The trailing threshold — a dynamic drawdown that moves upward as your account balance grows — can also confuse beginners who expect a fixed floor. Make sure you understand exactly how the trailing threshold works before starting your evaluation.

Best for: Futures traders who want a simple, single-phase path to funding without the risk of daily drawdown violations.

Want to see how Apex compares to other firms? Check out Apex vs AquaFunded, Apex vs Blue Guardian, and Apex vs BrightFunded for side-by-side breakdowns.

Read our full Apex Trader Funding review →

3. Topstep — Best for Risk-Conscious Futures Beginners

Rating: 4.2/5 | Starting at $165/mo

Topstep has been operating in the prop trading space since 2012 — longer than almost any firm competing for beginner traders today. That longevity matters. They've paid out millions to traders, refined their rules through multiple market cycles, and built a reputation for running a transparent, stable platform.

Like Apex, Topstep uses a one-step evaluation on futures markets. The evaluation requires you to hit a profit target while staying within their drawdown limits. The firm's signature feature is its strong emphasis on risk management — there's a consistency rule that encourages disciplined trading by preventing any single day from dominating your results.

The 100% profit split on your first $5,000 is a strong early incentive, shifting to 90% after that. While $165/month makes it the most expensive of our three picks, the track record and infrastructure justify the premium for traders who prioritize stability over lowest cost.

The main limitation: the consistency requirement can catch beginners off guard. If you have one exceptional day followed by average trading, the platform may flag your account. This is a feature, not a bug — it trains you to trade consistently — but it requires understanding before you start.

Best for: Futures traders who value a proven, long-established firm and want an evaluation process that builds real risk management discipline.

Read our full Topstep review →

How to Choose the Right Prop Firm as a Beginner

With dozens of prop firms competing for your evaluation fees, here's a practical framework for making a smart choice:

  • Start with your market: Do you trade forex? FTMO is the clear choice. Futures? Both Apex and Topstep excel. Don't force yourself into an unfamiliar market just because a firm has attractive terms.
  • Count the evaluation phases: One-step evaluations (Apex, Topstep) are more forgiving. Two-phase evaluations (FTMO) require sustained performance over a longer period but reward success with better long-term profit splits.
  • Read the drawdown rules twice: Know exactly what triggers a failed evaluation. Is there a daily drawdown limit? A trailing threshold? A maximum overall drawdown? These are the rules that end most beginners' evaluations — not poor trading.
  • Budget realistically: The monthly fee isn't your only cost. Budget for two to three evaluation attempts minimum when you're starting out. A $147/month plan that takes three attempts costs you $441 in fees — plan for that upfront.
  • Use every free trial available: FTMO offers a free trial of the evaluation process. Use it before paying. There is no reason to spend money on an evaluation you haven't tested.
  • Start with a smaller account size: Larger accounts cost more per month and often have larger absolute drawdown amounts, which means one bad trade does proportionally more damage. Start small, prove consistency, then scale up.

Common Mistakes Beginners Make at Prop Firms

Most failed evaluations aren't caused by bad trading strategy — they're caused by misunderstanding the rules. These are the most frequent beginner mistakes:

  • Misunderstanding the trailing threshold: Apex's trailing threshold moves upward as your account grows — it's not a fixed floor. Beginners often get stopped out mid-winning-streak because the threshold caught up to their balance.
  • Trading through major news events unprepared: NFP, FOMC, and CPI releases cause extreme volatility. A single news spike can breach a drawdown limit before you can react. Know the economic calendar before each session.
  • Chasing the profit target deadline: The pressure of a 30-day window causes beginners to overtrade and take oversized positions. This is the fastest way to fail an evaluation. Treat the deadline as irrelevant; focus on process.
  • Ignoring the consistency rule: Topstep's consistency requirement means no single day should dominate your results. A massive one-day gain followed by flat days can trigger a violation — even if your account is profitable overall.
  • Skipping the free trial: FTMO offers a free trial for a reason. Running through it at least once before paying removes almost all evaluation-rule surprises.
  • Comparing firms only on profit split: A 90% split is worthless if the drawdown rules make the evaluation nearly impossible to pass. Compare the full picture: rules, targets, drawdowns, and pricing together.

Our 2026 Recommendation: Which Prop Firm Should You Start With?

There's no single best prop firm for every beginner — the right answer depends on what you trade and how you trade it. Here's our honest guidance:

  • If you trade forex or want the broadest market access: Start with FTMO. Use the free trial first — no exceptions. The two-phase evaluation is more demanding, but the 90% profit split, fee refund on first payout, and extensive educational resources make it the best long-term value for serious beginners.
  • If you trade futures and want the simplest path to funding: Go with Apex Trader Funding. The one-step evaluation, no daily drawdown limit, and 100% profit split on your first $25,000 in profits make it exceptionally accessible at $147/month — the lowest price of our top three.
  • If you trade futures and prioritize long-term trust and structure: Choose Topstep. It's the most established firm in this space, and the consistency requirement will build trading discipline that serves you beyond just getting funded.

Regardless of which firm you choose, go in with a clear plan. Define your maximum position size before each session, set a personal daily loss limit even if the firm doesn't require one, and treat the evaluation as though it's live capital — because one day, it will be.

The traders who consistently get funded aren't necessarily the most talented. They're the most disciplined. Start with the smallest account size, learn every rule before you spend your first dollar, and use every free trial available. The path to a funded account is shorter than most beginners expect when they stop fighting the rules and start working with them.
